title: Back up and recover the local ledger
preconditions: The owner controls a permitted destination or backup artifact; the live Ledger can be opened or a restore target can be created.
postconditions: The owner has either a verified backup or a validated restored Ledger; the previous live Ledger was not silently overwritten.
main_flow: 1. The agent requests a point-in-time backup through a public command. 2. Tally produces an access-restricted artifact with format/schema identity. 3. Tally verifies the artifact without mutating the live Ledger and reports the result. 4. For recovery, the agent validates compatibility and integrity against a separate target. 5. Tally requires an explicit owner-authorized replacement step before overwriting a live Ledger. 6. The restored Ledger passes integrity checks and exposes the expected transaction counts/totals.
alt_flows: 2a. Destination permissions are unsafe or disk is full -> fail without claiming a valid backup
3a. Artifact verification fails -> mark it unusable and preserve the live Ledger
4a. Artifact is corrupt, incompatible or uses unavailable key material -> fail before replacement
5a. Replacement was not explicitly authorized -> restore only to a separate target or stop
6a. Restored integrity checks fail -> retain the previous live Ledger
metadata: {"depth_tier":3,"primary_actor":"P1","trigger":"The owner wants a recoverable backup, needs to verify one, or needs to recover after device, file, or migration failure.","status":"draft"}
